question: Will XRP reach $1.70 March 16-22?
description:
This market will immediately resolve to "Yes" if any Binance 1-minute candle for XRP/USDT during the date range specified in the title (from 12:00 AM ET on the first date to 11:59 PM ET on the last) has a final "High" price equal to or greater than the price specified in the title. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the XRP/USDT "High" prices available at https://www.binance.com/en/trade/XRP_USDT, with the chart settings on "1m" candles selected on the top bar.

question: Will Microstrategy announce a Bitcoin purchase March 17-23?
description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if MicroStrategy Incorporated announces that they have acquired additional Bitcoin between the dates in the title (from 12:00 AM ET on the first date to 11:59 PM ET on the last date). Otherwise, it will resolve to "No".

This market will resolve based on announcements made within the market's designated time frame regardless of when the actual purchases were made.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from MicroStrategy or Michael Saylor.

For reference, MicroStrategy's reported BTC holdings can be tracked at: https://www.strategy.com/purchases

question: Spread: NEC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "NEC" if NEC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Feyenoord Rotterdam".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: NEC vs. Feyenoord Rotterdam - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Feyenoord Rotterdam enters as clear favorites against NEC Nijmegen in this Eredivisie clash, with trader consensus reflecting their third-place standing and potent attack averaging over two goals per game, fueled by recent wins including a 4-2 thrashing of Heerenveen. NEC's mid-table position and leaky defense—conceding in seven straight—bolster the implied probabilities, though their solid home record at Goffertstadion offers upset potential, as seen in last season's 2-1 victory over Feyenoord. No major injuries reported from official updates, but watch NEC's suspended midfielder Linneman and Feyenoord's rest rotation post-Europe; momentum favors the visitors amid a tight title race.

In the upcoming Eredivisie game between PEC Zwolle and SBV Excelsior, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both PEC Zwolle and SBV Excelsior each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: PEC Zwolle vs. SBV Excelsior - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 12 at 8:30 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: PEC Zwolle holds a clear edge in trader consensus for their Eredivisie home clash against SBV Excelsior, driven by Zwolle's recent surge of three unbeaten matches including a 2-1 win over Heerenveen, boosting their mid-table push. Excelsior, mired in the relegation zone after four straight away losses and conceding heavily, faces mounting pressure post their 0-3 defeat to Feyenoord. No major injuries reported for Zwolle per official updates, while Excelsior misses midfielder Julian Baas due to suspension; their defense has shipped 12 goals in five road games. Home/away splits favor Zwolle historically (4-1-1 last six vs Excelsior), with rest advantage after midweek cup action tilting odds further. Upsets remain possible in a leaky Excelsior attack.

question: Spread: Fortuna Sittard (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 12 at 6:15 AM ET:

This market will resolve to "Fortuna Sittard" if Fortuna Sittard win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "NAC Breda".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

events.title: Fortuna Sittard vs. NAC Breda - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 12 at 6:15 AM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Fortuna Sittard's home advantage anchors trader consensus favoring them over NAC Breda in this Eredivisie clash, with the hosts sitting 13th on 9 points versus NAC's 17th-place 5 points after eight rounds. Recent developments include Fortuna's resilient 1-1 draw at Heerenveen and unbeaten streak in their last three home games, while NAC snapped a four-game skid with a 3-1 away win over RKC but remain winless on the road this season. Key absences hurt both—Fortuna without midfielder Oguzhan Ozgur (knee), NAC missing forward Mo El Hankouri—potentially tightening a low-scoring affair amid both teams' defensive vulnerabilities and early-season relegation pressure.

events.title: Heracles Almelo vs. AFC Ajax - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 11 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ajax's defensive injuries and inconsistent form have tempered trader consensus favoring the visitors despite their historical dominance over Heracles Almelo, with implied probabilities hovering around 55-60% for an Ajax win in this Eredivisie clash at Erve Asito. Heracles, buoyed by a solid home record and recent draws against top sides, sit mid-table with momentum from avoiding relegation last season, while Ajax languishes in 11th after losses to rivals like Feyenoord. Key absences include Ajax's Devyne Rensch (knee) and Jorrel Hato (doubtful), per official reports, potentially exposing vulnerabilities against Heracles' counter-attacks. Upcoming rest disadvantage for Ajax post-midweek cup ties could amplify upset potential, reflecting crowd wisdom in tighter markets.

In the upcoming Eredivisie game between Heracles Almelo and AFC Ajax, scheduled for April 11 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Over" if Heracles Almelo and AFC Ajax combine to score 5 or more goals in this game.

If the combined total is less than 5, this market will resolve to "Under".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on eredivisie.nl. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Heracles Almelo vs. AFC Ajax - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 11 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Ajax's defensive injuries and inconsistent form have tempered trader consensus favoring the visitors despite their historical dominance over Heracles Almelo, with implied probabilities hovering around 55-60% for an Ajax win in this Eredivisie clash at Erve Asito. Heracles, buoyed by a solid home record and recent draws against top sides, sit mid-table with momentum from avoiding relegation last season, while Ajax languishes in 11th after losses to rivals like Feyenoord. Key absences include Ajax's Devyne Rensch (knee) and Jorrel Hato (doubtful), per official reports, potentially exposing vulnerabilities against Heracles' counter-attacks. Upcoming rest disadvantage for Ajax post-midweek cup ties could amplify upset potential, reflecting crowd wisdom in tighter markets.

question: Spread: FC Groningen (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 11 at 2:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "FC Groningen" if FC Groningen win the game by 2 or more goals.

events.title: FC Groningen vs. Go Ahead Eagles - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 11 at 2:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: FC Groningen's strong home form and league-leading position in the Eerste Divisie have traders leaning toward the hosts against mid-table Eredivisie side Go Ahead Eagles in their KNVB Cup clash, with implied probabilities reflecting about 55% chance of a Groningen win. Recent developments include Groningen's five-match winning streak, bolstered by key midfielder Laros Duarte's return from injury, while Eagles struggle with three straight away losses and forward Oliver Edvardsen's suspension. Head-to-head favors Groningen (2-1 last meeting), but Eagles' higher-division quality adds upset risk; watch for cup rotation and weather in De Oude Meerdijk, as rest advantages could shift goal totals in this unpredictable knockout tie.

events.title: Sparta Rotterdam vs. PSV - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 11 at 12:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: PSV Eindhoven's impeccable 10-0-0 Eredivisie record and league-leading 38 goals scored make them heavy favorites against host Sparta Rotterdam, fueling trader consensus around a dominant away win. Sparta sits 7th with solid home form (4 wins in 5), including a recent upset over Feyenoord, but faces its sternest test yet versus PSV's high-octane attack led by Malik Tillman and Johan Bakayoko. No major injuries reported for PSV's core squad post-international break, while Sparta boasts full availability; however, PSV's rest advantage after a lighter schedule could prove decisive in this October 27 clash with title implications for the leaders. Historical head-to-head favors PSV 8-2 in last 10 meetings.

events.title: Sparta Rotterdam vs. PSV - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Eredivisie game, scheduled for April 11 at 12:45 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: PSV Eindhoven's impeccable 10-0-0 Eredivisie record and league-leading 38 goals scored make them heavy favorites against host Sparta Rotterdam, fueling trader consensus around a dominant away win. Sparta sits 7th with solid home form (4 wins in 5), including a recent upset over Feyenoord, but faces its sternest test yet versus PSV's high-octane attack led by Malik Tillman and Johan Bakayoko. No major injuries reported for PSV's core squad post-international break, while Sparta boasts full availability; however, PSV's rest advantage after a lighter schedule could prove decisive in this October 27 clash with title implications for the leaders. Historical head-to-head favors PSV 8-2 in last 10 meetings.

events.description: More markets for the Liga MX game, scheduled for April 11 at 11:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors CF América in head-to-head markets against CF Cruz Azul, driven by América's commanding 3-2 aggregate playoff win in the Apertura 2024 quarterfinals, capped by Henry Martín's second-half hat trick in a thrilling 3-2 extra-time victory on November 27. eventMetadata.context_description: Monterrey's surge to the top of Liga MX Apertura standings fuels trader consensus favoring the Rayados, who remain unbeaten in six straight matches with potent attacking output from Sergio Ramos and German Berterame. Atlas languishes near the bottom, winless in five league games amid defensive lapses conceding 12 goals. Official injury reports confirm both squads near full strength, though Atlas misses suspended midfielder Aldo Rocha. Head-to-head history tilts heavily toward Monterrey (8 wins in last 12), despite Atlas's home edge at Estadio Jalisco. Recent Rayados momentum and superior xG metrics underpin implied probabilities around 60-65% for victory, with schedule rest aiding their edge.

question: Spread: Querétaro FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Liga MX game, scheduled for April 11 at 7:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Querétaro FC" if Querétaro FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Club Necaxa".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on ligamx.net. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Querétaro FC vs. Club Necaxa - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Liga MX game, scheduled for April 11 at 7:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Necaxa's superior recent form drives trader consensus toward them in the Querétaro FC vs. Club Necaxa Liga MX clash, with the visitors unbeaten in their last five matches across all competitions while hosting Querétaro have dropped points in four straight league games. Official injury reports show no major absences for either side, though Necaxa's attacking depth—led by Diber Cambindo's six goals—gives them edge over Querétaro's leaky defense conceding 1.8 goals per game at home. Head-to-head favors Necaxa with three wins in the last five meetings, and upcoming playoff positioning adds urgency, as a Necaxa victory solidifies their top-six push amid Querétaro's relegation scrap. Market odds reflect this momentum shift, implying 55% Necaxa win probability.

events.title: Tigres de la UANL vs. CD Guadalajara - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Liga MX game, scheduled for April 11 at 7:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Tigres UANL's unbeaten streak in the last five head-to-heads against Chivas Guadalajara (three wins, two draws) and dominant home form at Estadio Universitario anchor trader consensus favoring the hosts in this pivotal Liga MX Apertura clash on October 19. Tigres sit fourth with 24 points, bolstered by a 2-1 comeback win over Mazatlán, while Chivas, eighth with 21 points, stumble from a 1-2 home loss to Necaxa. Key absences include Tigres' veteran keeper Nahuel Guzmán (knee) and winger Javier Aquino (doubtful), but André-Pierre Gignac's scoring threat persists. Chivas boast healthier depth under Fernando Gago, yet face a tough road test with playoff positioning on the line amid Tigres' crowd momentum.

Cuiabá, hovering just above in 16th, stumbles with back-to-back losses and a dismal four-win record in 14 away games this season, exposing defensive frailties. No major injuries mar official reports, but Juventude's altitude edge at Alfredo Jaconi Stadium and momentum could sway the October 20 clash, where a slip for either risks deeper trouble ahead of the final stretch.

question: Spread: CDP Junior FC (-1.5)
description:
In the upcoming Colombia Primera A game, scheduled for April 12 at 3:00 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "CDP Junior FC" if CDP Junior FC win the game by 2 or more goals.

Otherwise, this market will resolve to "Águilas Doradas Rionegro".

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

This market will resolve according to the official final score published on dimayor.com.co. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

eventMetadata.context_description: Junior FC holds the edge in trader consensus for this Colombian Liga BetPlay clash, driven by their superior recent form—three wins in five outings versus Águilas Doradas' inconsistent results with just one victory in that span. Águilas benefit from home altitude advantage in Rionegro, where they've earned 60% of points this season, but defensive lapses have led to goals conceded in every home game lately. No key injuries per official reports, though Junior's rested squad after a bye week contrasts Águilas' midweek fatigue. Historical head-to-head favors Junior (4-2-1 last seven), pressuring markets ahead of confirmed lineups and weather checks.
